assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md lines 30-33: 'Root Cause 1: Convention reference formatter (shared/convention-utils.md) — The convention reference formatter applies an incorrect case transformation (kebab-case) to CONVENTIONS.md section headings.' Lines 37-40: 'Root Cause 2: Task creation issue type (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a) — The task creation logic reads the Feature issue type ID (10142) from Jira Configuration instead of the Task issue type ID.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md lines 44-52: 'These two root causes are independent: They are in different modules: shared/convention-utils.md vs. plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a. They affect different code paths: convention reference formatting vs. Jira issue creation. This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6).' decomposition-guard.md line 13: 'These root causes are in different modules (shared/convention-utils.md vs. plan-feature/SKILL.md) and affect independent code paths.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 5-11: Issue 1 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and Issue 2 'Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)', each with file/module references.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 15-18: 'Options: 1. **Proceed** — create a single Task covering all fixes. 2. **Split** — I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually. Choose (1/2):'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md line 22: '**Execution paused.** Waiting for user input before proceeding. The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes.' No task-description.md or task creation output exists in eval-3 outputs, confirming the skill stopped.
